latest.title: Result of General Meeting and Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 10:32
RNS Number : 7144L
Ondo InsurTech PLC
09 July 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 (AS AMENDED) ("UK MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE. THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
Terms used but not defined in this announcement have the same meaning as set out in the Company's announcement released at 4:05 pm on
22 June
2026.
9 July 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo" or the "Company")
Result of General Meeting, Issue of Equity, Capital Reorganisation
and Total Voting Rights
Ondo InsurTech Plc (LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers, is pleased to announce that at the General Meeting ("
GM
") held earlier today, all resolutions, as set out in the GM notice published on 24 June 2026, were duly passed on a poll. Resolutions 1-3 were passed as ordinary resolutions and resolutions 4-6 were passed as special resolutions.
The results of the poll were as follows:
FOR
AGAINST
FOR
%
AGAINST
%
ABSTAIN

0.46
72,289
Completion of Fundraising and Capital Reorganisation
As a result of the resolutions being passed, the Company will allot 96,710,000 New Ordinary Shares comprising 87,039,000 Placing Shares and 9,671,000 Retail Offer Shares, subject to Admission, to raise gross proceeds of £2.9 million. The Company is also today issuing unsecured Convertible Loan Notes totalling £2.0 million.
As set out in the Circular and following the passing of the Resolutions, the Capital Reorganisation will now take effect to facilitate the issuance and Admission of the New Ordinary Shares pursuant to the Fundraising. Under the Capital Reorganisation, each Existing Ordinary Share of 5 pence nominal value will be subdivided and reclassified into one New Ordinary Share of 1 pence each and one new Deferred Share of 4 pence each.
Admission
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for 96,710,000 New Ordinary Shares to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange. It is expected that admission will become effective and that dealings in the New Ordinary Shares will commence at 8.00 a.m. on 10 July 2026.
The New Ordinary Shares, when issued, will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company, including, without limitation, the right to receive all dividends and other distributions declared, made or paid after the date of issue.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission of the New Ordinary Shares, the Company's issued share capital will comprise 246,524,760 Ordinary Shares all carrying one voting right each. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. Accordingly, the total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will be 246,524,760, which may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company pursuant to the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries
For further information, please visit www.ondoplc.com or contact the following:
Ondo InsurTech Plc
Mark Wood, Executive Chairman

latest.title: Share Incentive Plan Purchase
latest.content:
Today 16:20
RNS Number : 2347K
Ondo InsurTech PLC
29 June 2026
29 June 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo" or the "Company")
Share Incentive Plan Purchase
Director/PDMR Shareholding
Ondo InsurTech Plc (LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers, received notification on 29 June 2026 from Fiduchi Trustees Limited (UK) (the "SIP Trustee") in relation to the purchase of 57,410 ordinary shares of 5 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") for the benefit of participants of the Share Incentive Plan (the "SIP"), which includes Craig Foster and Kevin Withington, both Directors of the Company, outlined below in accordance with the rules of the SIP (the "SIP Award").
The SIP is an all-employee trust arrangement approved by HM Revenue and Customs, under which each participating employee will acquire Ordinary Shares at the prevailing market price per share using contributions deducted from pre-tax salary in each calendar month ("Partnership Shares"), with each participating employee being awarded one Ordinary Share for each Partnership Share ("Matching Shares") also acquired by the SIP.
Details of the number of Partnership Shares purchased by each of the Directors at a price of 7.0 pence per Ordinary Share, and the number of Matching Shares allocated by the SIP Trustee to the participants for no consideration, are set out against their names in the table below:

company_30s.what_they_do: Ondo Ltd. builds software that helps brands track, manage and protect their intellectual property and product integrity in the digital space. The platform pulls data from online marketplaces, social media and e‑commerce sites to spot counterfeit listings, unauthorized listings and supply‑chain leaks.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The online marketplace for branded goods is growing fast, and counterfeit activity costs brands billions each year. Ondo’s solution gives retailers and brands a real‑time view of where their products are being sold and who is selling them, giving them a competitive edge to protect margins and brand reputation.
company_30s.current_state: Ondo is still in a cash‑burn phase, raising capital to finish product development and scale sales. The company has just closed a share placing, launched a retail offer and is seeking further refinancing. The business is pre‑revenue and relies on a small but growing customer base.

rns_narrative: Ondo has moved from a small pilot phase into a formal funding round, raising almost £3 million through a new share placing at a low price. The company is also opening a retail offering, signalling it wants to broaden its shareholder base and bring in new capital.

In the same week, Ondo announced a refinancing and an equity fundraise to shore up liquidity and fund product development. The board has been refreshed with a new non‑executive director and a vendor loan note has been restructured, showing the company is actively managing its balance sheet.
These events create a clear pattern: the company is seeking cash to accelerate growth and has recently cleared the regulatory hurdles needed for a broader market offering.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious – the community is listening for the next funding move.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the retail offer and the new equity raise as a sign that the market is finally valuing the platform’s potential to grow into a large SaaS business.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many voices point out that Ondo still has no revenue, has taken on debt, and the placement price is low – a red flag that the company may need to keep diluting shareholders if it fails to hit milestones.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been largely flat, but recent posts about the retail launch suggest a slight uptick in optimism – a potential shift from pure silence to a “wait‑and‑see” stance.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows zero activity – the market has not yet paid attention, leaving the door open for surprise upside.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the retail share offering – a surge in volume could signal investor confidence.
Announcement of a new customer contract or pilot that shows product traction.
Finalization of the refinancing deal, which would improve liquidity and potentially allow the company to accelerate sales.
Any regulatory or partnership milestone that moves the company closer to revenue generation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in daily trade volume, a break above the recent low, and any press release that confirms a signed customer or partnership.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early phase of a potential mean‑reversion cycle – the recent crash has likely exhausted most weak hands. A clear support level has formed around the placement price, and the next move will test whether the market believes the company can deliver on its product promise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Ondo sits at a deep bottom – the placement price was set far below the market’s valuation of the underlying tech. If the company can close a significant customer or secure additional funding, the price could rebound to the placement level and beyond. Historically, deep crash signals deliver large upside once the catalyst triggers a bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has no revenue, carries a high debt load from the placing, and the equity raise will dilute existing shareholders. If it fails to win a key customer or the refinancing falls through, the price could remain trapped at the low.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: The downside is limited to the current trading price – you only risk what you pay. The upside, if the price reaches the placement level or higher, could be several times the entry price, offering a highly asymmetric risk‑reward profile.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the recent low, sizing the position to 5–10% of your discretionary capital. Place a stop just below the last swing low to protect against a false breakout. Use a trailing stop once the price moves 20% higher to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike that exceeds the average daily volume by at least 50% and a price break above the placement price. A confirmation of a new customer or a completed refinancing announcement would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks are evident at this stage; the company is still in a growth phase and not facing imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Ondo’s deep crash and upcoming funding moves create a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play – the next catalyst could trigger a strong mean‑reversion.
